title: 30o: compute_sha256_hex double-alloc via to_lowercase
description:
Location: crates/jmap-chat/src/blob.rs, compute_sha256_hex(), lines 191-197.

hex_digit() emits uppercase A-F (b'A' + nibble - 10). The fold builds a 64-char uppercase hex String, then .to_lowercase() allocates a second 64-char String and iterates all 64 bytes again. This is an avoidable allocation and O(n) second pass.

Fix: change the nibble-to-char mapping in compute_sha256_hex to emit lowercase directly (b'a' + nibble - 10), eliminating the .to_lowercase() call entirely.

Impact: one extra heap allocation and a second 64-byte scan per SHA-256 verification. Minor in absolute terms, but entirely unnecessary.
close_reason: fixed as part of 30o.17: to_lowercase() call removed
